\"\"\"\n251\t        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n252\t\n253\t        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n254\t        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n255\t        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n256\t        \"\"\"\n257\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n258\t updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n279\t            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n280\t            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n281\t                logger.warning(\n282\t                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n283\t                )\n284\t                return current_content, summary or {e}\")\n288\t            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n289\t\n290\t    def _estimate_confidence(\n291\t        self,\n292\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n293\t        change_summary: str\n294\t    ) -> float:\n295\t        \"\"\"\n296\t        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n297\t\n298\t        Higher confidence for:\n299\t        - Clear, specific instructions\n300\t        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n301\t        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n302\t        \"\"\"\n303\t        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n304\t\n305\t        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n306\t\n307\t        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n308\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n309\t            confidence += 0.2\n310\t\n311\t        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n312\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n313\t            confidence -= 0.2\n314\t\n315\t        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n316\t        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n317\t            confidence -= 0.1\n318\t\n319\t        # Clamp to valid range\n320\t        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n321\t\n322\t    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n323\t        \"\"\"\n324\t        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n325\t\n326\t        Args:\n327\t            diff: The diff to apply\n328\t\n329\t        Returns:\n330\t            True if successful, False otherwise\n331\t        \"\"\"\n332\t        try:\n333\t            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n334\t                return {e}\")\n342\t            return False\n343\t\n344\t    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n345\t        \"\"\"\n346\t        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n347\t\n348\t        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n349\t        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n350\t\n351\t        Args:\n352\t            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n353\t            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n354\t        \"\"\"\n355\t        try:\n356\t            # Get the repo root directory\n357\t            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n358\t\n359\t            # Get relative path from repo root\n360\t            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n361\t\n362\t            # Stage the file (only this specific file)\n363\t            subprocess.run(\n364\t                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n365\t                cwd=repo_root,\n366\t                capture_output=True,\n367\t                check=False,  # Don't fail if there are other uncommitted changes\n368\t                timeout=10\n369\t            )\n370\t\n371\t            # Create commit message with format: 'auto: self-mod write to <path>'\n372\t            commit_msg = f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path}\"\n373\t\n374\t            # Create the commit (only this specific file)\n375\t            result = subprocess.run(\n376\t                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg, '--', str(rel_path)],\n377\t                cwd=repo_root,\n378\t                capture_output=True,\n379\t                text=True,\n380\t                check=False,  # Don't fail if there are other uncommitted changes\n381\t                timeout=10\n382\t            )\n383\t\n384\t            # Only log if commit was successful\n385\t            if result.returncode == 0:\n386\t                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n387\t                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n388\t                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n389\t                    cwd=repo_root,\n390\t                    capture_output=True,\n391\t                    text=True,\n392\t                    check=True,\n393\t                    timeout=10\n394\t                )\n395\t\n396\t                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n397\t                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n398\t            else:\n399\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n400\t\n401\t        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n402\t            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n403\t        except Exception as e:\n404\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n405\t\n406\t    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n407\t        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n408\t        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n409\t        if not artifact:\n410\t            return False\n411\t\n412\t        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n413\t            f.write(diff.after)\n414\t\n415\t        # Force reload to pick up changes\n416\t        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n417\t\n418\t        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n419\t        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n420\t\n421\t        return True\n422\t\n423\t    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) False\n428\t\n429\t        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n430\t            f.write(diff.after)\n431\t\n432\t        # Force reload\n433\t        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n434\t\n435\t        # Create git commit for the config write\n436\t        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path,  # For components, we need to identify the component\n443\t        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n444\t        if not diff.change_summary\n455\t        )\n456\t        return True\n457\t\n458\t    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n459\t        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n460\t        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n461\t            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n462\t\n463\t    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n464\t        \"\"\"\n465\t        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n466\t\n467\t        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n468\t        For components: use component version history\n469\t        \"\"\"\n470\t        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n471\t            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n472\t\n473\t        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n474\t        import subprocess\n475\t        try:\n476\t            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n477\t            if not artifact:\n478\t                return False\n479\t\n480\t            # Get previous version from git\n481\t            result = subprocess.run(\n482\t                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n483\t                capture_output=True,\n484\t                text=True,\n485\t                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n486\t            )\n487\t\n488\t            if result.returncode == 0:\n489\t                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n490\t                    f.write(result.stdout)\n491\t                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n492\t                return True\n493\t        except Exception as e:\n494\t            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n495\t\n496\t        return False\n497\t\n498\t    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n499\t        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n500\t        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n501\t        if not component or component.version <= Agent for aide-de-camp.\n\nReads and writes artifacts (prompts, configs) to improve system behavior\nbased on user feedback.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport time\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List, Tuple\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\nfrom ..components.library import get_library\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Prompt paths read per-invocation so edits take effect without a server restart\n# (hot-reload), matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py and\n# src/intent/router.py.\nSELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_parse.md\")\nSELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_generate.md\")\n\n# Fallbacks used only if a prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You classify a user instruction to the artifact it targets. \"\n    'Return ONLY JSON: {\"artifact_type\": \"prompt|config|component\", '\n    '\"artifact_name\": \"<name>\", \"reasoning\": \"...\"}.'\n)\n_GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You apply a user instruction to an artifact. Return ONLY JSON: \"\n    '{\"updated_content\": \"<full updated text>\", \"change_summary\": \"one sentence\"}.'\n)\n\n\nclass ArtifactType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Types of artifacts that can be modified.\"\"\"\n    PROMPT = \"prompt\"\n    CONFIG = \"config\"\n    COMPONENT = \"component\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ArtifactDiff:\n    \"\"\"A diff showing changes to an artifact.\"\"\"\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: ArtifactType\n    before: str\n    after: str\n    change_summary: str\n    confidence: float\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ModificationRequest:\n    \"\"\"A user request to modify system behavior.\"\"\"\n    instruction: str\n    artifact_name: Optional[str]\n    artifact_type: Optional[ArtifactType]\n    context: Dict[str, Any]\n\n\nclass SelfModificationAgent:\n    \"\"\"\n    Agent that modifies system artifacts based on user feedback.\n\n    Workflow:\n    1. Receive user instruction\n    2. Identify target artifact\n    3. Read current artifact content\n    4. Generate update\n    5. Surface diff to user\n    6. On approval: write artifact\n    7. On rejection: discard\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        parse_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n        generate_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n    ):\n        self.reload_mgr = get_reload_manager()\n        self.component_library = get_library()\n        self._pending_diffs: List[ArtifactDiff] = []\n        self.parse_prompt_path = parse_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH\n        self.generate_prompt_path = generate_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the ZAI proxy client (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    def _load_prompt(self, path: Path, fallback: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load a self-modification prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load prompt {path}: {e}\")\n            return fallback\n\n    def _available_artifacts(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Build the list of registered artifacts for the parser prompt.\"\"\"\n        artifacts: List[Dict[str, str]] = []\n        for name, path_str in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().items():\n            suffix = Path(path_str).suffix.lower()\n            type_str = \"config\" if suffix in (\".yaml\", \".yml\") else \"prompt\"\n            artifacts.append({\"name\": name, \"type\": type_str})\n        return artifacts\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _strip_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Strip ```json ... ``` markdown fences from a GLM response.\"\"\"\n        raw = raw.strip()\n        if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n            raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n            raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n        return raw\n\n    async def process_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ArtifactDiff:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a user instruction for system modification.\n\n        Args:\n            instruction: Natural language instruction\n\n        Returns:\n            The proposed diff for user approval\n        \"\"\"\n        # Parse the instruction to identify target (LLM call)\n        request = await self._parse_instruction(instruction)\n\n        # Get current content\n        current_content = self._get_artifact_content(request)\n\n        # Generate update (LLM call)\n        updated_content, change_summary = await self._generate_update(\n            request,\n            current_content\n        )\n\n        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n            artifact_name=request.artifact_name or \"unknown\",\n            artifact_type=request.artifact_type or ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=current_content,\n            after=updated_content,\n            change_summary=change_summary,\n            confidence=self._estimate_confidence(request, change_summary)\n        )\n\n        self._pending_diffs.append(diff)\n        return diff\n\n    async def _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n\n        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, _PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK)\n        user_message = (\n            \"## Registered Artifacts\\n\"\n            + json.dumps(self._available_artifacts(), indent=2)\n            + f\"\\n\\n## User Instruction\\n{instruction}\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # cheap, fast classification\n                max_tokens=512,\n                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            type_str = data.get(\"artifact_type\", \"prompt\")\n            name = data.get(\"artifact_name\")\n\n            try:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType(type_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            # Validate the name is actually registered; fall back to a known\n            # artifact (preferring the router prompt) so we never target a\n            # non-existent artifact.\n            registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n            registered_set = set(registered)\n            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n                not name or name not in registered_set\n            ):\n                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n                    name = \"router\"\n                elif registered:\n                    name = registered[0]\n                else:\n                    name = \"unknown\"\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=name,\n                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n                context={\n                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                },\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: {e}\"\n            )\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=\"router\",\n                artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n            )\n\n    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: ModificationRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current content of the target artifact.\"\"\"\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                return self.reload_mgr.get_prompt(request.artifact_name)\n        elif request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                # For configs, return YAML as string for diff\n                artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n                if artifact:\n                    return artifact.content\n\n        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n\n    async def _generate_update(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        current_content: str\n    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n\n        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n        )\n        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n        user_message = (\n            f\"## Instruction\\n{request.instruction}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Artifact Type\\n{artifact_type}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Current Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.2,\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n                )\n                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n\n    def _estimate_confidence(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        change_summary: str\n    ) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n\n        Higher confidence for:\n        - Clear, specific instructions\n        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n        \"\"\"\n        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n\n        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n\n        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n            confidence += 0.2\n\n        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n            confidence -= 0.2\n\n        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            confidence -= 0.1\n\n        # Clamp to valid range\n        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n\n    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n\n        Args:\n            diff: The diff to apply\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successful, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n                return self._write_prompt(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n                return self._write_config(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n                return self._write_component(diff)\n            return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Failed to apply diff: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n\n        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n\n        Args:\n            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get the repo root directory\n            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n\n            # Get relative path from repo root\n            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n\n            # Stage the file (only this specific file)\n            subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                check=False,  # Don't fail if there are other uncommitted changes\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            # Create commit message with format: 'auto: self-mod write to <path>'\n            commit_msg = f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path}\"\n\n            # Create the commit (only this specific file)\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg, '--', str(rel_path)],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,  # Don't fail if there are other uncommitted changes\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            # Only log if commit was successful\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n                    cwd=repo_root,\n                    capture_output=True,\n                    text=True,\n                    check=True,\n                    timeout=10\n                )\n\n                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n\n    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload to pick up changes\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated config file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the config write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_component(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated component to library.\"\"\"\n        # For components, we need to identify the component\n        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n        if not diff.artifact_name.startswith(\"comp-\"):\n            return False\n\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not component:\n            return False\n\n        self.component_library.update_component(\n            component.id,\n            diff.after,\n            diff.change_summary\n        )\n        return True\n\n    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n\n    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n\n        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n        For components: use component version history\n        \"\"\"\n        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n\n        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n        import subprocess\n        try:\n            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n            if not artifact:\n                return False\n\n            # Get previous version from git\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n            )\n\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n                    f.write(result.stdout)\n                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n\n        return False\n\n    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n        if not component or component.version <= 1:\n            return False\n\n        target_version = component.version - 1\n        self.component_library.rollback_component(component_id, target_version)\n        return True\n\n    def list_pending_diffs(self) -> List[ArtifactDiff]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending diffs awaiting approval.\"\"\"\n        return self._pending_diffs.copy()\n\n    def clear_pending_diffs(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all pending diffs.\"\"\"\n        self._pending_diffs.clear()\n\n\n# Singleton instance\n_agent: Optional[SelfModificationAgent] = None\n\n\ndef get_self_modification_agent() -> SelfModificationAgent:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the self-modification agent singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _agent\n    if _agent is None:\n        _agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n    return _agent\n","numLines":527,"startLine":1,"totalLines":527}}}

as e:\n        return GitResult(\n            success=False,\n            stdout=\"\",\n            stderr=str(e),\n            returncode=-1,\n            timed_out=False\n        )\n\n\ndef git_status(cwd: Optional[Path] = None, short: bool = True) -> GitResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run git status.\n\n    Args:\n        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n        short: If True, use --short format (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        GitResult with status output\n    \"\"\"\n    args = ['status', '--short'] if short else ['status']\n    return run_git_command(args, cwd=cwd)\n\n\ndef git_add(paths: List[str], cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stage files for commit.\n\n    Args:\n        paths: List of file paths to stage (relative to cwd)\n        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n\n    Returns:\n        GitResult with add output\n    \"\"\"\n    args = ['add'] + paths\n    return run_git_command(args, cwd=cwd)\n\n\ndef git_commit(message: str, paths: Optional[List[str]] = None, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a git commit.\n\n    Args:\n        message: Commit message\n        paths: Optional list of specific paths to commit (default: all staged)\n        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n\n    Returns:\n        GitResult with commit output\n    \"\"\"\n    args = ['commit', '-m', message]\n    if paths is not None:\n        args.extend(['--'] + paths)\n    return run_git_command(args, cwd=cwd)\n\n\ndef git_show(ref: str, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Show git object content (e.g., 'HEAD:path/to/file').\n\n    Args:\n        ref: Git reference (e.g., aide-de-camp.\n\nReads and writes artifacts (prompts, configs) to improve system behavior\nbased on user feedback.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport time\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List, Tuple\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\nfrom ..components.library import get_library\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Prompt paths read per-invocation so edits take effect without a server restart\n# (hot-reload), matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py and\n# src/intent/router.py.\nSELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_parse.md\")\nSELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_generate.md\")\n\n# Fallbacks used only if a prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You classify a user instruction to the artifact it targets. \"\n    'Return ONLY JSON: {\"artifact_type\": \"prompt|config|component\", '\n    '\"artifact_name\": \"<name>\", \"reasoning\": \"...\"}.'\n)\n_GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You apply a user instruction to an artifact. Return ONLY JSON: \"\n    '{\"updated_content\": \"<full updated text>\", \"change_summary\": \"one sentence\"}.'\n)\n\n\nclass ArtifactType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Types of artifacts that can be modified.\"\"\"\n    PROMPT = \"prompt\"\n    CONFIG = \"config\"\n    COMPONENT = \"component\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ArtifactDiff:\n    \"\"\"A diff showing changes to an artifact.\"\"\"\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: ArtifactType\n    before: str\n    after: str\n    change_summary: str\n    confidence: float\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ModificationRequest:\n    \"\"\"A user request to modify system behavior.\"\"\"\n    instruction: str\n    artifact_name: Optional[str]\n    artifact_type: Optional[ArtifactType]\n    context: Dict[str, Any]\n\n\nclass SelfModificationAgent:\n    \"\"\"\n    Agent that modifies system artifacts based on user feedback.\n\n    Workflow:\n    1. Receive user instruction\n    2. Identify target artifact\n    3. Read current artifact content\n    4. Generate update\n    5. Surface diff to user\n    6. On approval: write artifact\n    7. On rejection: discard\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        parse_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n        generate_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n    ):\n        self.reload_mgr = get_reload_manager()\n        self.component_library = get_library()\n        self._pending_diffs: List[ArtifactDiff] = []\n        self.parse_prompt_path = parse_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH\n        self.generate_prompt_path = generate_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the ZAI proxy client (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    def _load_prompt(self, path: Path, fallback: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load a self-modification prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load prompt {path}: {e}\")\n            return fallback\n\n    def _available_artifacts(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Build the list of registered artifacts for the parser prompt.\"\"\"\n        artifacts: List[Dict[str, str]] = []\n        for name, path_str in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().items():\n            suffix = Path(path_str).suffix.lower()\n            type_str = \"config\" if suffix in (\".yaml\", \".yml\") else \"prompt\"\n            artifacts.append({\"name\": name, \"type\": type_str})\n        return artifacts\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _strip_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Strip ```json ... ``` markdown fences from a GLM response.\"\"\"\n        raw = raw.strip()\n        if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n            raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n            raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n        return raw\n\n    async def process_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ArtifactDiff:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a user instruction for system modification.\n\n        Args:\n            instruction: Natural language instruction\n\n        Returns:\n            The proposed diff for user approval\n        \"\"\"\n        # Parse the instruction to identify target (LLM call)\n        request = await self._parse_instruction(instruction)\n\n        # Get current content\n        current_content = self._get_artifact_content(request)\n\n        # Generate update (LLM call)\n        updated_content, change_summary = await self._generate_update(\n            request,\n            current_content\n        )\n\n        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n            artifact_name=request.artifact_name or \"unknown\",\n            artifact_type=request.artifact_type or ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=current_content,\n            after=updated_content,\n            change_summary=change_summary,\n            confidence=self._estimate_confidence(request, change_summary)\n        )\n\n        self._pending_diffs.append(diff)\n        return diff\n\n    async def _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n\n        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, _PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK)\n        user_message = (\n            \"## Registered Artifacts\\n\"\n            + json.dumps(self._available_artifacts(), indent=2)\n            + f\"\\n\\n## User Instruction\\n{instruction}\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # cheap, fast classification\n                max_tokens=512,\n                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            type_str = data.get(\"artifact_type\", \"prompt\")\n            name = data.get(\"artifact_name\")\n\n            try:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType(type_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            # Validate the name is actually registered; fall back to a known\n            # artifact (preferring the router prompt) so we never target a\n            # non-existent artifact.\n            registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n            registered_set = set(registered)\n            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n                not name or name not in registered_set\n            ):\n                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n                    name = \"router\"\n                elif registered:\n                    name = registered[0]\n                else:\n                    name = \"unknown\"\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=name,\n                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n                context={\n                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                },\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: {e}\"\n            )\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=\"router\",\n                artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n            )\n\n    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: ModificationRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current content of the target artifact.\"\"\"\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                return self.reload_mgr.get_prompt(request.artifact_name)\n        elif request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                # For configs, return YAML as string for diff\n                artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n                if artifact:\n                    return artifact.content\n\n        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n\n    async def _generate_update(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        current_content: str\n    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n\n        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n        )\n        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n        user_message = (\n            f\"## Instruction\\n{request.instruction}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Artifact Type\\n{artifact_type}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Current Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.2,\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n                )\n                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n\n    def _estimate_confidence(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        change_summary: str\n    ) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n\n        Higher confidence for:\n        - Clear, specific instructions\n        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n        \"\"\"\n        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n\n        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n\n        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n            confidence += 0.2\n\n        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n            confidence -= 0.2\n\n        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            confidence -= 0.1\n\n        # Clamp to valid range\n        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n\n    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n\n        Args:\n            diff: The diff to apply\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successful, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n                return self._write_prompt(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n                return self._write_config(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n                return self._write_component(diff)\n            return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Failed to apply diff: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n\n        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n\n        Args:\n            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get the repo root directory\n            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n\n            # Get relative path from repo root\n            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n\n            # Stage the file (only this specific file)\n            subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                check=False,  # Don't fail if there are other uncommitted changes\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            # Create commit message with format: 'auto: self-mod write to <path>'\n            commit_msg = f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path}\"\n\n            # Create the commit (only this specific file)\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg, '--', str(rel_path)],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,  # Don't fail if there are other uncommitted changes\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            # Only log if commit was successful\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n                    cwd=repo_root,\n                    capture_output=True,\n                    text=True,\n                    check=True,\n                    timeout=10\n                )\n\n                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n\n    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload to pick up changes\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated config file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the config write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_component(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated component to library.\"\"\"\n        # For components, we need to identify the component\n        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n        if not diff.artifact_name.startswith(\"comp-\"):\n            return False\n\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not component:\n            return False\n\n        self.component_library.update_component(\n            component.id,\n            diff.after,\n            diff.change_summary\n        )\n        return True\n\n    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n\n    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n\n        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n        For components: use component version history\n        \"\"\"\n        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n\n        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n        import subprocess\n        try:\n            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n            if not artifact:\n                return False\n\n            # Get previous version from git\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n            )\n\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n                    f.write(result.stdout)\n                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n\n        return False\n\n    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n        if not component or component.version <= 1:\n            return False\n\n        target_version = component.version - 1\n        self.component_library.rollback_component(component_id, target_version)\n        return True\n\n    def list_pending_diffs(self) -> List[ArtifactDiff]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending diffs awaiting approval.\"\"\"\n        return self._pending_diffs.copy()\n\n    def clear_pending_diffs(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all pending diffs.\"\"\"\n        self._pending_diffs.clear()\n\n\n# Singleton instance\n_agent: If a pod has more than 3 restarts, \"\n            \"classify urgency as high.\",\n        )\n\n        calls = []\n\n        async def fake_call_simple(system_prompt, user_message, **kwargs):\n            calls.append(kwargs.get(\"model\"))\n            if kwargs.get(\"model\") == ModelClass.HAIKU.value:\n                # Parse call\n                return json.dumps(\n                    {\n                        \"artifact_type\": \"prompt\",\n                        \"artifact_name\": \"urgency\",\n                        \"reasoning\": \"urgency-related\",\n                    }\n                )\n            # Generate call\n            return json.dumps(\n                {\n                    \"updated_content\": modified,\n                    \"change_summary\": \"Added high-urgency rule for pods with >3 restarts\",\n                }\n            )\n\n        agent._zai_client = MagicMock()\n        agent._zai_client.call_simple = fake_call_simple\n\n        diff = await agent.process_instruction(NONTRIVIAL_INSTRUCTION)\n\n        # Create initial content\n        test_prompt_file.write_text(\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nInitial content.\")\n\n        # Create agent and register the prompt\n        agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n        mgr = HotReloadManager()\n        mgr.register_prompt(\"test_git_commit_prompt\", str(test_prompt_file))\n        agent.reload_mgr = mgr\n\n        # Create a diff representing a change\n        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n            artifact_name=\"test_git_commit_prompt\",\n            artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nInitial content.\",\n            after=\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nUpdated content for git commit test.\",\n            change_summary=\"Update test prompt for git commit\",\n            confidence=0.9\n        )\n\n        # Get initial commit count\n        initial_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        initial_count = int(initial_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Apply the diff (which should create a git commit)\n        result = agent._write_prompt(diff)\n\n        # Verify the write was successful\n        assert result is True\n\n        # Verify git commit was created\n        final_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        final_count = int(final_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Should have at least one more commit\n        assert final_count >= initial_count + 1\n\n        # Verify commit message format in recent commits\n        recent_commits = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"log\", \"--oneline\", \"-5\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        commit_history = recent_commits.stdout.strip()\n\n        assert \"auto: self-mod write to\" in commit_history\n        assert \"test_git_commit_prompt\" in commit_history\n\n    def test_write_config_creates_git_commit(self, test_config_file):\n        \"\"\"Writing a config file creates a git commit.\"\"\"\n        # Get initial commit count\n        initial_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        initial_count = int(initial_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Should have at least one more commit\n        assert final_count >= initial_count + 1\n\n        # Verify commit message format in recent commits\n        recent_commits = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"log\", \"--oneline\", \"-5\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        commit_history = recent_commits.stdout.strip()\n\n        assert \"auto: self-mod write to\" in commit_history\n        assert \"test_git_commit_config\" in commit_history\n","numLines":415,"startLine":1,"totalLines":415}}}

If a pod has more than 3 restarts, \"\n            \"classify urgency as high.\",\n        )\n\n        captured = {}\n\n        async def fake_call_simple(system_prompt, user_message, **kwargs):\n            captured[\"model\"] = kwargs.get(\"model\")\n            captured[\"user_message\"] = user_message\n            return json.dumps(\n                {\n                    \"updated_content\": modified,\n                    \"change_summary\": \"Treat pods with >3 restarts as high urgency\",\n                }\n            )\n\n        agent._zai_client = MagicMock()\n        agent._zai_client.call_simple = fake_call_simple\n\n        request = ModificationRequest(\n            instruction=NONTRIVIAL_INSTRUCTION,\n            artifact_name=\"urgency\",\n            artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            context={},\n        )\n        updated, summary = await agent._generate_update(request, original)\n\n        # A real LLM call was made (SONNET for rewriting) carrying the current\n        # content and the instruction.\n        assert captured[\"model\"] == ModelClass.SONNET.value\n        assert NONTRIVIAL_INSTRUCTION in captured[\"user_message\"]\n        assert original in captured[\"user_message\"]\n\n        # If a pod has more than 3 restarts, \"\n            \"classify urgency as high.\",\n        )\n\n        calls = []\n\n        async def fake_call_simple(system_prompt, user_message, **kwargs):\n            calls.append(kwargs.get(\"model\"))\n            if kwargs.get(\"model\") == ModelClass.HAIKU.value:\n                # Generate call\n            return json.dumps(\n                {\n                    \"updated_content\": modified,\n                    \"change_summary\": \"Added high-urgency rule for pods with >3 restarts\",\n                }\n            )\n\n        agent._zai_client = MagicMock()\n        agent._zai_client.call_simple = fake_call_simple\n\n        diff = await agent.process_instruction(NONTRIVIAL_INSTRUCTION)\n\n        # Two distinct LLM calls: one to parse, one to generate.\n        assert calls == [ModelClass.HAIKU.value, ModelClass.SONNET.value]\n\n        assert diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT\n        assert diff.artifact_name == \"urgency\"\n        assert diff.before == original\n        assert diff.after == modified\n        assert diff.after != original\n\n        # Create initial content\n        test_prompt_file.write_text(\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nInitial content.\")\n\n        # Create agent and register the prompt\n        agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n        mgr = HotReloadManager()\n        mgr.register_prompt(\"test_git_commit_prompt\", str(test_prompt_file))\n        agent.reload_mgr = mgr\n\n        # Create a diff representing a change\n        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n            artifact_name=\"test_git_commit_prompt\",\n            artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nInitial content.\",\n            after=\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nUpdated content for git commit test.\",\n            change_summary=\"Update test prompt for git commit\",\n            confidence=0.9\n        )\n\n        # Get initial commit count\n        initial_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        initial_count = int(initial_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Apply the diff (which should create a git commit)\n        result = agent._write_prompt(diff)\n\n        # Verify the write was successful\n        assert result is True\n\n        # Verify git commit was created\n        final_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        final_count = int(final_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Should have at least one more commit\n        assert final_count >= initial_count + 1\n\n        # Verify commit message format in recent commits\n        recent_commits = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"log\", \"--oneline\", \"-5\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        commit_history = recent_commits.stdout.strip()\n\n        assert \"auto: self-mod write to\" in commit_history\n        assert \"test_git_commit_prompt\" in commit_history\n\n    def test_write_config_creates_git_commit(self, test_config_file):\n        \"\"\"Writing a config file creates a git commit.\"\"\"\n        # 'git' with no arguments should fail\n        result = run_git_command(['invalid-subcommand'])\n\n        assert result.success is False\n        assert result.returncode != 0\n        assert result.timed_out is False\n\n    def test_run_git_command_timeout(self):\n        \"\"\"run_git_command handles timeouts.\"\"\"\n        # Use a very short timeout; git operations shouldn't normally timeout\n        result = run_git_command(['status'], timeout=0.001)\n\n        assert result.success is False\n        assert result.timed_out is True\n\n    def test_git_status_executes_and_returns_output(self):\n        \"\"\"git_status executes 'git status' and returns output.\"\"\"\n        result = git_status(short=True)\n\n        assert result.success is True\n        assert result.returncode == 0\n        assert isinstance(result.stdout, str)\n\n    def test_git_status_long_format(self):\n        \"\"\"git_status supports long format output.\"\"\"\n        result = git_status(short=False)\n\n        assert result.success is True\n        assert result.returncode == 0\n        # Long format should have more verbose output\n        assert len(result.stdout) > 0\n\n    def test_git_add_files(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"git_add stages files for commit.\"\"\"\n        # Create a test file\n        test_file = tmp_path / \"test_add.txt\"\n        test_file.write_text(\"test content\")\n\n        result = git_add([str(test_file)], cwd=tmp_path)\n\n        assert result.success is True\n        assert result.returncode == 0\n\n    def test_git_commit_creates_commit(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"git_commit creates a commit with a message.\"\"\"\n        # Initialize a git repo\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'init'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'config', 'user.email', 'test@example.com'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'config', 'user.name', 'Test User'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True)\n\n        # Create and stage a file\n        test_file = tmp_path / \"test_commit.txt\"\n        test_file.write_text(\"test content\")\n        git_add([str(test_file)], cwd=tmp_path)\n\n        # Create commit\n        result = git_commit(\"Test commit message\", cwd=tmp_path)\n\n        assert result.success is True\n        assert result.returncode == 0\n\n    def test_git_commit_with_specific_paths(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"git_commit can commit specific paths.\"\"\"\n        # Initialize a git repo\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'init'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'config', 'user.email', 'test@example.com'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'config', 'user.name', 'Test User'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True)\n\n        # Create and stage multiple files\n        file1 = tmp_path / \"file1.txt\"\n        file2 = tmp_path / \"file2.txt\"\n        file1.write_text(\"content 1\")\n        file2.write_text(\"content 2\")\n\n        git_add([str(file1), str(file2)], cwd=tmp_path)\n\n        # Commit only file1\n        result = git_commit(\"Commit file1\", paths=[\"file1.txt\"], cwd=tmp_path)\n\n        assert result.success is True\n        assert result.returncode == 0\n\n    def test_git_rev_parse_short_sha(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"git_rev_parse returns short SHA.\"\"\"\n        # call.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_produces_substantive_diff_not_boilerplate(self, agent, temp_urgency_md):\n        original = agent.reload_mgr.get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n\n        # Simulate the LLM making a real, on-topic edit to the prompt body.\n        modified = original.replace(\n            \"Pods restarting repeatedly.\",\n            \"Pods restarting repeatedly. If a pod has more than 3 restarts, \"\n            \"classify urgency as high.\",\n        )\n\n        captured = {}\n\n        async def fake_call_simple(system_prompt, user_message, **kwargs):\n            captured[\"model\"] = kwargs.get(\"model\")\n            captured[\"user_message\"] = user_message\n            return json.dumps(\n                {\n                    \"updated_content\": modified,\n                    \"change_summary\": \"Treat pods with >3 restarts as high urgency\",\n                }\n            )\n\n        agent._zai_client = MagicMock()\n        agent._zai_client.call_simple = fake_call_simple\n\n        request = ModificationRequest(\n            instruction=NONTRIVIAL_INSTRUCTION,\n            artifact_name=\"urgency\",\n            artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            context={},\n        )\n        updated, summary = await agent._generate_update(request, original)\n\n        # Should not fabricate a change.\n        assert updated == original\n\n\nclass TestProcessInstructionEndToEnd:\n    \"\"\"The full instruction → diff pipeline uses two real LLM calls.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_nontrivial_instruction_yields_on_topic_diff(self, agent, temp_urgency_md):\n        original = agent.reload_mgr.get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        modified = original.replace(\n            \"Pods restarting repeatedly.\",\n            \"Pods restarting repeatedly. If a pod has more than 3 restarts, \"\n            \"classify urgency as high.\",\n        )\n\n        calls = []\n\n        async def fake_call_simple(system_prompt, user_message, **kwargs):\n            calls.append(kwargs.get(\"model\"))\n            if kwargs.get(\"model\") == ModelClass.HAIKU.value:\n                # Parse call\n                return json.dumps(\n                    {\n                        \"artifact_type\": \"prompt\",\n                        \"artifact_name\": \"urgency\",\n                        \"reasoning\": \"urgency-related\",\n                    }\n                )\n            # Generate call\n            return json.dumps(\n                {\n                    \"updated_content\": modified,\n                    \"change_summary\": \"Added high-urgency rule for pods with >3 restarts\",\n                }\n            )\n\n        agent._zai_client = MagicMock()\n        agent._zai_client.call_simple = fake_call_simple\n\n        diff = await agent.process_instruction(NONTRIVIAL_INSTRUCTION)\n\n        # Two distinct LLM calls: one to parse, one to generate.\n        assert calls == [ModelClass.HAIKU.value, ModelClass.SONNET.value]\n\n        assert diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT\n        assert diff.artifact_name == \"urgency\"\n        assert diff.before == original\n        assert diff.after == modified\n        assert diff.after != original\n\n        # Defining regression check: the result is a substantive, on-topic diff,\n        # NOT the old generic \"# User feedback: ...\" comment append that the\n        # heuristic stub would have produced for this instruction.\n        assert \"# User feedback:\" not in diff.after\n        assert \"3 restarts\" in diff.after\n\n\nclass TestGitCommits:\n    \"\"\"Git commits are created for prompt and config writes.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.fixture\n    def test_prompt_file(self):\n        \"\"\"Create a test prompt file in the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n        test_prompt_path = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/test_git_commit_prompt.md\")\n        original_content = test_prompt_path.read_text() if test_prompt_path.exists() else None\n\n        yield test_prompt_path\n\n        # Cleanup: remove the test file (git keeps it in history)\n        if test_prompt_path.exists():\n            test_prompt_path.unlink()\n\n    @pytest.fixture\n    def test_config_file(self):\n        \"\"\"Create a test config file in the config directory.\"\"\"\n        test_config_path = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/config/test_git_commit_config.yaml\")\n        original_content = test_config_path.read_text() if test_config_path.exists() else None\n\n        yield test_config_path\n\n        # Cleanup: remove the test file (git keeps it in history)\n        if test_config_path.exists():\n            test_config_path.unlink()\n\n    def test_write_prompt_creates_git_commit(self, test_prompt_file):\n        \"\"\"Writing a prompt file creates a git commit.\"\"\"\n        # Create initial content\n        test_prompt_file.write_text(\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nInitial content.\")\n\n        # Create agent and register the prompt\n        agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n        mgr = HotReloadManager()\n        mgr.register_prompt(\"test_git_commit_prompt\", str(test_prompt_file))\n        agent.reload_mgr = mgr\n\n        # Create a diff representing a change\n        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n            artifact_name=\"test_git_commit_prompt\",\n            artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nInitial content.\",\n            after=\"# Test Prompt\\n\\nUpdated content for git commit test.\",\n            change_summary=\"Update test prompt for git commit\",\n            confidence=0.9\n        )\n\n        # Get initial commit count\n        initial_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        initial_count = int(initial_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Apply the diff (which should create a git commit)\n        result = agent._write_prompt(diff)\n\n        # Verify the write was successful\n        assert result is True\n\n        # Verify git commit was created\n        final_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        final_count = int(final_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Should have at least one more commit\n        assert final_count >= initial_count + 1\n\n        # Verify commit message format in recent commits\n        recent_commits = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"log\", \"--oneline\", \"-5\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        commit_history = recent_commits.stdout.strip()\n\n        assert \"auto: self-mod write to\" in commit_history\n        assert \"test_git_commit_prompt\" in commit_history\n\n    def test_write_config_creates_git_commit(self, test_config_file):\n        \"\"\"Writing a config file creates a git commit.\"\"\"\n        # Create initial content\n        test_config_file.write_text(\"key: value\\noriginal: true\")\n\n        # Create agent and register the config\n        agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n        mgr = HotReloadManager()\n        mgr.register_prompt(\"test_git_commit_config\", str(test_config_file))\n        agent.reload_mgr = mgr\n\n        # Create a diff representing a change\n        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n            artifact_name=\"test_git_commit_config\",\n            artifact_type=ArtifactType.CONFIG,\n            before=\"key: value\\noriginal: true\",\n            after=\"key: new_value\\noriginal: false\",\n            change_summary=\"Update test config for git commit\",\n            confidence=0.8\n        )\n\n        # Get initial commit count\n        initial_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        initial_count = int(initial_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        # Apply the diff (which should create a git commit)\n        result = agent._write_config(diff)\n\n        # Verify the write was successful\n        assert result is True\n\n        # Verify git commit was created\n        final_commit_count = subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"rev-list\", \"--count\", \"HEAD\"],\n            cwd=Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"),\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True\n        )\n        final_count = int(final_commit_count.stdout.strip())\n\n        #
